name: fzf
description: A general-purpose command-line fuzzy finder. It is an interactive Unix filter for command-line that can be used with any list: files, command history, processes, hostnames, bookmarks, git commits, etc.

globalOptions.name: --extended
globalOptions.description: Extended-search mode. Since 0.10.9, this is enabled by default. You can disable it with +x or --no-extended

globalOptions.name: --exact
globalOptions.description: Enable exact-match mode. In this mode, fzf only matches the exact search string

globalOptions.name: --algo
globalOptions.description: Fuzzy matching algorithm. v2 (default) is optimized for common use cases; v1 uses a simpler but faster algorithm

globalOptions.name: --multi
globalOptions.description: Enable multi-select with tab/shift-tab. Optionally specify the maximum number of selections with -m N

globalOptions.name: --no-mouse
globalOptions.description: Disable mouse support

globalOptions.name: --bind
globalOptions.description: Custom key bindings. Refer to the man page for the full list of available actions. e.g. --bind 'ctrl-j:accept,ctrl-k:kill-line'

globalOptions.name: --cycle
globalOptions.description: Enable cyclic scroll, allowing the cursor to wrap around from the end of the list to the beginning and vice versa

globalOptions.name: --keep-right
globalOptions.description: Keep the right end of the line visible when it is too long. Effective only when the query string is empty

globalOptions.name: --scroll-off
globalOptions.description: Number of screen lines to keep above or below when scrolling to the top or to the bottom

globalOptions.name: --no-hscroll
globalOptions.description: Disable horizontal scroll of the preview window

globalOptions.name: --filepath-word
globalOptions.description: Make word-wise movements and actions respect path separators. The following actions are affected: backward-kill-word, backward-word, forward-word, kill-word

globalOptions.name: --jump-labels
globalOptions.description: Label characters for jump and jump-accept actions

globalOptions.name: --height
globalOptions.description: Display fzf window below the cursor with the given height instead of using the full screen. Accepts percentage value (e.g. --height 40%) or the number of lines

globalOptions.name: --min-height
globalOptions.description: Minimum height when --height is given in percent. Ignored when --height is not specified

globalOptions.name: --layout
globalOptions.description: Choose the layout: 'default' (from the bottom of the screen), 'reverse' (from the top), 'reverse-list' (prompt at the bottom)

globalOptions.name: --border
globalOptions.description: Draw border around the finder. Style can be: rounded, sharp, bold, double, horizontal, vertical, top, bottom, left, right, none

globalOptions.name: --margin
globalOptions.description: Comma-separated expression for margins around the finder. e.g. --margin 1,5 or --margin 1,2,3,4 (top, right, bottom, left)

globalOptions.name: --padding
globalOptions.description: Comma-separated expression for padding inside the border. Same format as --margin

globalOptions.name: --info
globalOptions.description: Determines the display style of the finder info. default: display on the next line, inline: display on the same line, hidden: do not display

globalOptions.name: --prompt
globalOptions.description: Input prompt string (default: '> ')

globalOptions.name: --pointer
globalOptions.description: Pointer to the current line (default: '>')

globalOptions.name: --marker
globalOptions.description: Multi-select marker (default: '>')

globalOptions.name: --header
globalOptions.description: The given string will be printed as the sticky header. The lines are displayed in the given order from top to bottom

globalOptions.name: --header-lines
globalOptions.description: The first N lines of the input are treated as the sticky header

globalOptions.name: --header-first
globalOptions.description: Print header before the prompt line

globalOptions.name: --ansi
globalOptions.description: Enable processing of ANSI color codes in the input

globalOptions.name: --tabstop
globalOptions.description: Number of spaces for a tab character (default: 8)

globalOptions.name: --color
globalOptions.description: Color configuration. Base scheme and/or custom color mappings. e.g. --color=dark or --color='fg:12,bg:0,hl:4'

globalOptions.name: --no-bold
globalOptions.description: Do not use bold text

globalOptions.name: --history
globalOptions.description: Load search history from the specified file and update on exit. When enabled, ctrl-n and ctrl-p navigate through history

globalOptions.name: --history-size
globalOptions.description: Maximum number of entries in the history file (default: 1000). The file is automatically truncated when the number exceeds the limit

globalOptions.name: --preview
globalOptions.description: Execute the given command for the current line and display the result in the preview window. {} in the command is replaced with the single-quoted string of the current line

globalOptions.name: --preview-window
globalOptions.description: Determines the layout of the preview window. e.g. --preview-window=up:40%:wrap or --preview-window=right:60%:hidden

globalOptions.name: --preview-label
globalOptions.description: Label to print on the border of the preview window

globalOptions.name: --query
globalOptions.description: Start the finder with the given initial query

globalOptions.name: --select-1
globalOptions.description: If there is only one match for the initial query, do not start the interactive finder and automatically select the only match

globalOptions.name: --exit-0
globalOptions.description: If there is no match for the initial query, do not start the interactive finder and exit immediately

globalOptions.name: --filter
globalOptions.description: Filter mode. Do not start the interactive finder. Applied to each line of input and print matches in order of decreasing relevance

globalOptions.name: --print-query
globalOptions.description: Print query as the first line of the output

globalOptions.name: --expect
globalOptions.description: Comma-separated list of keys that can be used to complete fzf in addition to the default enter key

globalOptions.name: --read0
globalOptions.description: Read input delimited by ASCII NUL characters instead of newline characters

globalOptions.name: --print0
globalOptions.description: Print output delimited by ASCII NUL characters instead of newline characters

globalOptions.name: --sync
globalOptions.description: Synchronous search for multi-staged filtering. fzf will wait for the input to finish before starting the finder

globalOptions.name: --listen
globalOptions.description: Start HTTP server on the given port and allow remote actions via HTTP

globalOptions.name: --walker
globalOptions.description: Comma-separated list of walker options: file, dir, follow, hidden. Controls what the built-in filesystem walker includes

globalOptions.name: --walker-root
globalOptions.description: Root directory from which the built-in filesystem walker starts. Default is the current directory

globalOptions.name: --walker-skip
globalOptions.description: Comma-separated list of directory names to skip when walking the filesystem. e.g. --walker-skip .git,node_modules

globalOptions.name: --delimiter
globalOptions.description: Field delimiter regex for --nth and --with-nth (default: AWK-style)

globalOptions.name: --with-nth
globalOptions.description: Comma-separated list of field index expressions for limiting the display. Fields are determined by --delimiter

globalOptions.name: --nth
globalOptions.description: Comma-separated list of field index expressions for limiting search scope. Each can be a non-zero integer or a range expression (e.g. 1, 2..5, ..-1)

globalOptions.name: --sort
globalOptions.description: Sort the result. Optionally specify the number of entries to sort (default: 1000). +s or --no-sort to disable sorting

globalOptions.name: --tac
globalOptions.description: Reverse the order of the input

globalOptions.name: --disabled
globalOptions.description: Do not perform search. Useful for turning fzf into an interactive selector with a custom filtering mechanism via --bind and reload action

globalOptions.name: --tiebreak
globalOptions.description: Comma-separated list of sort criteria to apply when the scores are tied. Available criteria: length, chunk, begin, end, index (default: length)

globalOptions.name: --phony
globalOptions.description: Deprecated alias for --disabled
